claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my two children. Their names are Alex and Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! It's lovely to meet you. Let's go through some questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake.

First, could you tell me how old Alex and Blake are?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 8 years old, and Blake is 20.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Since Blake is 20, I need to ask a follow-up question about them. Is Blake currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, NVQs, or certain apprenticeships)? For Child Benefit to continue past age 16, the child generally needs to be in qualifying full-time education or approved training.

And just to confirm for Alex who is 8 - does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Alex does live with me, yes. 

As for Blake, no, they're not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

I have a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Are you responsible for Alex's day-to-day care (such as providing food, clothing, and a home)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, I do have Alex living with me, but actually their other parent also lives with us and already claims Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that important detail. That helps clarify the situation for Alex.

Now, I just have one more question to confirm Blake's circumstances. You mentioned Blake is 20 and not in education or training. Just to be thorough - did Blake leave education or training recently, and if so, when did they finish?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake left education a couple of years ago, so it wasn't recent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all the information I needed. Based on the UK government rules, here is my assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit:

---

### **Alex (age 8)** – ❌ **Not eligible for you to claim**

Although Alex lives with you and is under 16, Child Benefit is already being claimed by their other parent. Under the rules, **only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child at any one time**. Since Alex's other parent is already receiving Child Benefit, you cannot make a separate claim.

If you and the other parent would like to change who receives the Child Benefit, you would need to contact the Child Benefit Office to arrange this.

---

### **Blake (age 20)** – ❌ **Not eligible**

Blake is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. To qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or over, they must remain in **approved education or training** (such as A-levels, NVQs, or certain apprenticeships). Since Blake left education a couple of years ago and is no longer in qualifying education or training, they no longer meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

---

### **Summary**

Unfortunately, based on your circumstances, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake at this time.

If you have any further questions or your circumstances change, please feel free to reach out. I hope this has been helpful!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and lives with you, which would normally make you eligible for Child Benefit. However, you have confirmed that Alex's other parent, who also lives in your household, is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Under the rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child at any one time. Since Child Benefit is already being claimed for Alex by someone else, you are not eligible to make a separate claim for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old and left education a couple of years ago. To qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or over, the child must remain in approved education or training until they turn 20. Since Blake is not in any form of approved education or training and has not been for some time, they no longer meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Based on the information provided, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for either child. For Alex (age 8), Child Benefit is already being claimed by their other parent who lives with you, and only one person can receive Child Benefit per child. For Blake (age 20), they are over 16 and not in approved education or training, which means they no longer qualify for Child Benefit.
